meta.notes: Figure-read MAEs (CHCOH pathway, C1 pathway, OER) extracted from prose annotations of figures. Several abstract claims duplicate body/table values (prose_vs_table candidates). Tier proposals: most comparative MAE claims are T2 (internal cross-consistency between prose and tables/figures); literature claims about other models are T5.

claims.text: FS models with 5% to 10% of perturbed (high-energy) configurations from MD or contour exploring can reduce the error by over 2x compared to models trained only on relaxation trajectories.

location.quote:
that FS models with 5 % to 10 % of perturbed (high-energy) configurations from
MD or contour exploring (CE), can reduce the error by over 2× compared to
models trained only on relaxation trajectories.

claims.predicate: error_reduction_factor >= 2 when 5-10% perturbed configs added vs relaxation-only

claims.text: An MLIP fine-tuned on metallic catalysts achieves an MAE of 0.30 eV for OER on iridium oxide polymorphs, which is 0.08 eV lower than out-of-box MACE-MH-1 and 0.14 eV lower than the best performing FS MLIP.

location.quote:
an MLIP fine-tuned on metallic catalysts achieves an MAE
of 0.30 eV for OER on iridium oxide polymorphs, which is 0.08 eV lower than
out-of-box MACE-MH-1 and 0.14 eV lower than the best performing FS MLIP.

claims.predicate: reported_MAE_OER == 0.30 eV; diff_vs_MACE_MH1 == 0.08 eV; diff_vs_best_FS == 0.14 eV

claims.text: An MLIP fine-tuned to O and OH adsorption on metal oxides gives an Ea MAE of 0.19 eV for out-of-distribution CO2RR reaction on Cu.

location.quote:
an MLIP fine-tuned to O and OH adsorption on metal oxides gives
an Ea MAE of 0.19 eV for out-of-distribution CO2RR reaction on Cu

claims.predicate: reported_Ea_MAE == 0.19 eV for FT-MD(MO) on CO2RR Cu

claims.text: A large MLIP fine-tuned to 49,860 configurations achieves an MAE of 0.15 eV for Er when screening a left-out set of 90,781 bimetallic alloys.

location.quote:
a large MLIP fine-tuned to 49,860
configurations shows the best performance across both metallic and metal-oxide
catalysts, which was used to screen a large-left out, 90,781, of bimetallic alloys,
achieving an MAE of 0.15 eV for Er

claims.scope: FT-All on 90,781 bimetallic alloy screening, 2,115 DFT test subset

claims.text: The FS-BMA model achieves an MAE of 0.659 eV for the metal test set Er.

location.quote:
achieves
an MAE of 0.659 eV when predicting the metal test set Er

claims.scope: FS-BMA, metallic catalyst Er test set

claims.text: The FS-BMA+MD model achieves an MAE of 0.310 eV for metallic catalyst Er and 0.476 eV for Er(MO).

location.quote:
the
model achieves an MAE of 0.310 eV for metallic catalyst Er, better than the performance of the unmodified large foundation models (UMA and MACE-MH-1), but
performs poorly for Er(MO) with an MAE of 0.476 eV

claims.predicate: reported_Er_M_MAE == 0.310 eV; reported_Er_MO_MAE == 0.476 eV for FS-BMA+MD

claims.text: The FS-All model achieves an MAE of 0.175 eV for Ea(M) predictions, about half of the error of the FS-BMA+MD model.

location.quote:
This model achieves an MAE
of 0.175 eV for Ea(M) predictions, about half of the error calculated using the
FS-BMA+MD model, within 0.05 eV of the UMA and MACE-MH-1 models’ MAE.

claims.predicate: reported_Ea_M_MAE == 0.175 eV for FS-All; ~= 0.5 * FS-BMA+MD Ea MAE (0.398)

claims.text: FT-BMA (relaxation trajectories only) achieves Er(MO) MAE of 0.334 eV, about 0.06 eV lower than a model with about 2,692 metal-oxide configurations in training.

location.quote:
with
no metal oxide in the training set, the Er(MO) MAE is 0.334 eV, about 0.06 eV lower
than a model that had about 2,692 metal-oxides configurations in the training set.

claims.predicate: reported_FT_BMA_Er_MO_MAE == 0.334 eV; diff vs FT-MD(MO) (0.306) ~= 0.06 eV

claims.text: The FT-MD(M) model performs worse than the FT-BMA model with a 0.15 eV increase in Er(M) MAE.

location.quote:
The FT-MD(M) model performs worse than the FT-BMA model
with a 0.15 eV increase in Er(M) MAE.

claims.text: The FT-MD(M) model Er(MO) MAE is 0.428 eV while the FT-BMA MAE is 0.334 eV.

location.quote:
the FT-MD(M) model MAE for Er(MO) is 0.428 eV
while the FT-BMA MAE is 0.334 eV

claims.text: The FT-CE model performs better for Er(MO) (MAE = 0.305 eV) than FT-BMA and FT-MD(M), almost identical to FT-MD(MO) (MAE = 0.306 eV).

location.quote:
performs better for Er(MO) (MAE = 0.305 eV) than
the FT-BMA and FT-MD(M) models. It also has almost identical performance to the
FT-MD(MO) model (MAE = 0.306 eV)

claims.text: FT-CE was fine-tuned using 1,789 Cu surface structures and 420 Ag, Ir, Pd, Pt, and Rh surface structures generated using CE.

location.quote:
the FT-CE model, which was
fine-tuned using 1,789 Cu surface structures and 420 Ag, Ir, Pd, Pt, and Rh surface
structures generated using CE

claims.text: After fine-tuning on all datasets (FT-All), the Er(MO) MAE decreases by 0.07 eV.

location.quote:
the Er(M) and Ea (M) MAE
are unchanged, the Er(MO) MAE decreases by 0.07 eV.

claims.predicate: FT_MD_MO_Er_MO (0.306) - FT_All_Er_MO (0.277) ~= 0.07 eV OR baseline diff
claims.scope: FT-All vs prior FT models, Er metal-oxide

claims.text: For the CHCOH* pathway CO2RR reconstruction on Cu(001), MACE-MH-1 (OMAT) has MAE 1.011 eV, FS-BMA 0.455 eV, FS-All 0.251 eV, and FT-All 0.141 eV across 7 reactions.

location.quote:
MACE-MH-1 (OMAT) performs the worst with an MAE of 1.011 eV. The
FS-BMA model exhibits an error of of up to 0.5 eV for each elementary step (overall
MAE of 0.455 eV)

claims.predicate: reported MAEs: MH1=1.011, FS-BMA=0.455, FS-All=0.251, FT-All=0.141 eV

claims.text: FT-All achieves an MAE of 0.141 eV across the 7 CHCOH* reactions and 0.143 eV across the entire 101 reactions reported across other catalysts.

location.quote:
the best performing model
with an MAE of 0.141 eV across these 7 reactions, and similarly achieve an MAE of
0.143 eV across the entire 101 reactions reported across other catalysts in this study.

claims.text: The FT-All model predicts CO protonation to C1 products with an MAE of 0.124 eV with respect to DFT.

location.quote:
The FT-All model
(solid lines) predicts the energy of each step with an MAE of 0.124 eV with respect to
the DFT values

claims.scope: FT-All, CO protonation to C1 products

claims.text: Tang et al. provide 23 DFT NEB reactions used as an evaluation set.

location.quote:
Their work provides 23 DFT NEB
reactions created using similar DFT settings as those used to train our MLIPs

claims.text: The 0.2 eV higher barrier prediction error by the FS-MD model arises because it predicted a different initial state, not the transition state.

location.quote:
the 0.2 eV higher
error in predicting the barrier by the FS-MD model arises because it has predicted
a different initial state as compared to DFT, not the transition state.

claims.predicate: FS-MD barrier error ~0.2 eV attributed to initial-state difference

claims.text: Applying FT-All to final optimized NEB images showed a difference of 0.3 eV in barrier predictions.

location.quote:
observed a difference of 0.3 eV
in barrier predictions, indicating a high sensitivity of Ea to the structures at the initial
and transitions states.

claims.scope: FT-All single-point on NEB final images

claims.text: Flores et al. calculated OER energetics for a total of 96 reactions on rutile, alpha, and beta phases of IrO2 and IrO3.

location.quote:
calculated the OER energetics for a total of 96 reactions on rutile (R), α,
and β phases of several different surface terminations of IrO2 and IrO3 under applied
potential.

claims.text: The FS-All model has an error of 0.21 eV for O and OH adsorption but 1.6 eV for OOH* adsorption on alpha-IrO3(110); overall MAE 0.438 eV for the 96 reactions.

location.quote:
has an error of 0.21 eV for O and OH adsorption. However,
the error is significantly higher for OOH* adsorption at 1.6 eV, which is attributed
to the absence of OOH* species in the training set. For the 96 reactions reported in
Ref. [58], the FS-All model has an MAE of 0.438 eV.

claims.predicate: FS_All_O_OH_error == 0.21 eV; FS_All_OOH_error == 1.6 eV; FS_All_OER_MAE == 0.438 eV
claims.scope: FS-All, OER on alpha-IrO3(110) and 96 reactions

claims.text: Using MACE-MH-1 reduces OOH* error to less than 0.5 eV with an overall MAE of 0.384 eV, 0.05 eV lower than FS-All.

location.quote:
reduces
the error for OOH* to less than 0.5 eV, and has an over-
all MAE of 0.384 eV, which is a 0.05 eV lower than FS-All model.

claims.predicate: MACE_MH1_OER_MAE == 0.384 eV; diff vs FS-All (0.438) ~= 0.05 eV

claims.text: The total MAEs for the 96 OER reactions for FT-MD(MO) and FT-All models are 0.313 and 0.278 eV, respectively.

location.quote:
The total MAEs for the
96 reaction for FT-MD(MO) and FT-All models are 0.313 and 0.278 eV, respectively,
both showing better performance than the from-scratch models.

claims.text: The screening procedure produced 90,781 total initialized structures, of which 90,181 converged.

location.quote:
This procedure produced 90,781 total initialized structures,
of which 90,181 converged.

claims.text: For 2,115 DFT test configurations, the FT-All model adsorption energy MAE is 0.15 eV.

location.quote: For these 2,115 configurations, the FT-All model adsorption energy MAE is 0.15 eV

claims.text: For the (311) surface termination, 77% of configurations are within ±0.2 eV; for (532) facet only 58% are within threshold; for all other terminations 65% to 75% lie within ±0.2 eV.

location.quote:
For the (311) surface termination, 77% of configurations are within the target
threshold of ±0.2 eV. The accuracy is lowest for the (532) facet, the highest surface
termination we considered, with only 58% of predictions within the error threshold.
Nevertheless for all other surface terminations, 65% to 75% of the tested configurations
lie within with the ±0.2 eV tolerance.

claims.scope: FT-All adsorption energy by surface termination

claims.text: For AgNi and CuAu, 68% and 46% are within ±0.2 eV of DFT respectively; for CuIr, 73% are within the threshold.

location.quote:
For AgNi and CuAu, the performance is mediocre
with only 68% and 46% within ±0.2 eV of the DFT values, respectively. On the other
hand, the model performance is much better for CuIr, with 73% within the threshold.

claims.scope: FT-All adsorption energy by surface composition

claims.text: The FT-All model has an MAE of 0.278 eV for OER on IrO polymorphs, better than the from-scratch model (MAE = 0.384 eV).

location.quote:
FT-All model has an MAE
of 0.278 eV, noticeably better the error of from-scratch model (MAE = 0.384 eV).

claims.text: 81% of a randomly selected subset of reactions had less than ±0.2 eV error with respect to DFT values for adsorption energy screening.

location.quote:
with 81% of randomly selected subset of reactions having
less than ±0.2 eV error with respect to the DFT values.

claims.text: For Er of CHCOH pathway for the CO2RR, the FT-All model achieves an MAE of 0.141 eV vs FS-All MAE of 0.251 eV.

location.quote:
the FT-All model achieves an MAE of 0.141 eV, noticeably better than our
best from scratch model (FS-All MAE = 0.251 eV).

claims.text: All MLIPs fine-tuned to different training sets have a MAE of between 0.14 to 0.15 eV for Ea NEB calculations, better than the best FS model (MAE = 0.175 eV).

location.quote:
all our
MLIPs fine-tuned to a different training set each have a MAE of between 0.14 to
0.15 eV, better than our best performing from scratch model (MAE = 0.175 eV).

claims.scope: FT models Ea NEB, Ref [62] reactions

claims.text: Wood et al. UMA model is able to estimate Er within 0.1 eV of DFT for 74% of Er reported in the AdsorbML task.

location.quote:
is able to estimate Er within 0.1 eV of DFT, for 74
% of Er reported in AdsorbML task.

claims.predicate: UMA estimates Er within 0.1 eV for 74% of AdsorbML Er
claims.scope: Wood et al. UMA, AdsorbML task

claims.text: The Batatia et al. fine-tuned model has an MAE of 0.095 eV for S24 and 0.138 eV for OC20 benchmark tests.

location.quote:
with an MAE of 0.095 eV for the S24 [25] and 0.138 eV for OC20
benchmark tests.

claims.scope: Batatia et al. multi-head replay model

claims.text: CatTSunami MLIP-predicted NEBs were successful for 56% of the 900 reactions studied, improving to 86% when ∆Ea calculated using single-point DFT energies.

location.quote:
They found that the MLIP-
predicted NEBs were successful for 56% of the 900 reactions studied, which improved
to 86% when the ∆Ea were calculated using single-point DFT energies at the MLIP’s
initial, transition, and final state structures.

claims.text: Schaaf et al. trained an MLIP with MAE of 0.05 eV for Ea for CO2 hydrogenation to methanol on In2O3 in six active-learning loops.

location.quote:
trained an accurate MLIP (MAE of 0.05 eV
for Ea) for CO2 hydrogenation to methanol on In2O3 in six active-learning loops
